deploy.ci: .github/workflows/deploy.yml runs npm ci, npm test, npm run build on node 22 and publishes dist to GitHub Pages
deploy.netlify: retired and dead; every historical Netlify site and the npx netlify deploy route is out of the deploy path and must not be used
deploy.approvalRule: no push or deploy without Robbie's explicit approval after local verification is summarized

contentEpochNote: Launch L1 0.99.2 shipped the content epoch as a real per-run runtime stamp (route-run.contentEpoch, save v8), separate from mapSchema (generator structure) and saveSchema (serialized shape). genMap resolves treasure pools, district power, and the enchant pool through epoch tables; the current epoch computes live so today's maps stay byte-identical. A future content or balance change bumps CONTENT_EPOCH and freezes the epoch it leaves into EPOCH_TABLES, so a supported active run regenerates its own epoch's map instead of being retired. readRouteSave retires only on a mapSchema mismatch.

activeProject.name: Manor Identity
activeProject.outcome: Production implementation of the approved haunted-manor visual system from STYLE.md and the composite proof: asset filing through the established art pipeline, presentation foundation, then staged screen rebuilds. One presentation system per version; engine, saves, balance, and route semantics untouched.
activeProject.note: Ten manor versions 0.102.0 to 0.113.0 on local main. The furniture pass moved the fight from CSS chrome to painted chrome per Robbie references. Robbie fal balance roughly 8 dollars. Deploy waits on Robbie.

reservations.scope: The furniture pass, answering Robbie 50 percent verdict that CSS chrome missed the reference bar: engraved brass bar housings on all health bars via 9-slice, carved oval frame rings on fight portraits, the carved frame on the dealer crest, and the first full-body hero spectacle layer (Kilnkeeper with burning chain) stamped by hero id in fights. Assets generated, cut, QA approved; drape strip rejected at QA. No engine or timing change.
reservations.verification: npm test 559 green via ship.js. Live Souk Rats fight verified at 390x844: housings, oval rings, crest frame, and the full-body dealer all render; overlap of his own board first cell accepted as reference-style staging. Follow-up recorded: full-body art for the other seven heroes, drape retry, route path gilding.

reservations.scope: The complete painted catalog: the 20 unpainted wares (payoff, R7, and district additions) generated via style-anchored edit chains, cut out, QA approved on a contact sheet, and filed. 82 of 82 items and 69 of 69 monsters now painted.
reservations.verification: npm test 559 green. Live market verified: Bellows Boy and siblings render painted ports. Ingest matcher predates these ids so art-prep filed them; matcher refresh recorded as follow-up.

reservations.scope: Landscape threshold scenes (wide manor gates intro, wide gallery market) and the stall panel made translucent so the painted hallway breathes through.
reservations.verification: npm test 559 green after filing. Both wides are same-slot overrides; landscape is the declared primary orientation.

reservations.scope: Victory and defeat staging per the proof: the recap cards become result bands with the hero lit large, a one-line consequence, and the bounty anticipated before returning to the route; defeat mirrors with cold light and the Resolve cost. Presentation markup and CSS in the fight end cards only; rewards, spoils, receipts, and route consequences untouched.
reservations.verification: npm test 559 green, layout 62 passed with only the documented pre-existing Omen-count failures. The recap leads with the dealer lit face and one consequence line; damage detail folds behind Fight details.

reservations.scope: Event rooms: the five generated scenes (parlor, hearth nook, seance, treasure attic, cellar gate) file as ui assets and back the Merchant, Rest, Shrine, Treasure, and Gate Camp surfaces so each decision happens in its own room instead of a generic card. Overlay backdrop CSS and asset filing only; choice logic, offers, receipts, and exact-once semantics untouched.
reservations.verification: npm test 559 of 559 green via ship.js. Playwright layout suite 62 passed, 2 failed on the documented pre-existing stale Omen-count assertion from 0.98.0 (expects 12 Omens, game has 16), unrelated to this diff. Five room scenes filed as ui assets with whitelist; Merchant, Rest, Shrine, and Treasure cards carry room classes threaded through choiceCard with zero choice or settlement change. Gate Camp room deferred: its render is a separate surface, recorded for the polish version.

reservations.scope: Combat recomposition per the approved proof: hero and foe become large lit anchors in the fight headers, the space between boards becomes a real action lane, the always-open log demotes behind a toggle on portrait, and the pocket-watch bezel files as a ui asset ringing fight cells. Presentation markup and CSS only; the engine, timing, targeting, and event stream untouched.
reservations.verification: npm test 559 of 559 green via ship.js. Live Lamp Imp fight at 390x844: 64px side-lit hero and foe anchors with Rakkas names, the action lane takes the portrait flex so the ballroom shows between boards, the log is a 46px tap-to-open strip. Deviation recorded: the circular pocket-watch bezel asset does not suit the rounded-square fight cells, so cells keep the glowing conic ring; the bezel remains reserved for a future circular dial surface. No engine or timing change.

reservations.scope: Hero crest HUD: the draft ribbon becomes the dealer crest from the approved proof, hero portrait plus name plus favors line anchored left with Resolve and Gold chips attached, Tier and Omen demoted to a secondary strip. Presentation markup and CSS in ui.js ribbon render and index.html; no state, economy, or save change.
reservations.verification: npm test 559 of 559 green via ship.js. reservations.startGate: CLEAR. Gate 1 (Codex independently signs 0.100.0) is MET: Codex signed 0.100.0 on 2026-07-18. Gate 2 (Codex reviews and approves the final 0.101.0 handoff) is MET: Codex approved the final handoff at commit 7335c4b80ea17d36c2d07ab68e76d701cde3be48 on 2026-07-18 (approval codex-approved-0.101.0-handoff-2026-07-18). Both gates are clear, so the difficulty worker may now begin 0.101.0 on the reserved branch launch-calibrated-challenge-0.101.0 and worktree C:/tmp/bazaar-brawler-l2-0.101.0, following the approved handoff. This unblock changes no approved scope, file set, constant, or handoff specification.
reservations.note: Reserved by the main integrator (Claude) as a handoff to the difficulty worker. Both start gates are now clear (see startGate); source work has not started yet but the difficulty worker may now begin. Reservation is anchored to canonical HEAD 5d2570b, whose ancestry contains the integrated 0.100.0 commit 6dbf27e. The dedicated branch launch-calibrated-challenge-0.101.0 and worktree C:/tmp/bazaar-brawler-l2-0.101.0 are created by the main integrator with no source changes; the difficulty worker is the sole writer of src/data.js for 0.101.0. Claude integrates the verified source and finalizes this state; Codex signs the evidence gate and does not implement the constants.
reservations.scope: Resolve-attrition curve reshape (difficulty worker owns the curve), corrected per Codex's rejection of the 2b933bc handoff. Primary trial: Azhdaha hasteMates 0.5 to exactly 0.30 on the base board, azhdaha_v1, and azhdaha_v2; Quick D2 power 1.12 and D3 power 1.18; Long D5 power 1.6 to 2.10. HELD in the primary trial: Long D6 power at 2.05 (D6 2.15 is a rejection control only, never the primary; if 2.05 stays storm-dominated, test a lower D6 separately) and vizier hp at 700 (660 only if the complete paired matrix proves it necessary, not optional polish). No hasteMates 0.25 lever. No scout-card or storm-clock telegraph in this version. District power (Quick D2, D3, Long D5) is an epoch-resolved map input, so it bumps CONTENT_EPOCH 1 to 2 and freezes epoch 1 into EPOCH_TABLES rather than retiring active runs (supersedes the design doc's MAP_VERSION save-retirement). The Azhdaha hasteMates change is an original-monster combat stat needing a parity-ledger entry; there is NO combat-golden regeneration (the fixtures are synthetic and reference no monster board, and the synthetic hasteMates 0.5 fixture must remain unchanged because it pins engine semantics). The hasteMates change moves the azhdaha_v1 and azhdaha_v2 traces (0.100.0 controls), which must re-confirm in band at 2,400 seeds. The difficulty worker must implement a new pure, deterministic, DOM-free verifier scripts/launch-l2-verify.mjs (route-sim output is insufficient) that runs live hero, Omen, shop, unlock, and combat rules (hero and Omen evidence is in scope, not out of scope), exits nonzero on any failed gate, invalid run, guard trip, timeout, route guard exit, pending action, unsupported active-run retirement, malformed artifact, or sample-count shortfall, and writes three JSON artifacts (baseline, candidate, comparison). The quantitative contract is in the handoff: expected Resolve bands with strictly falling whole-cohort point estimates and ended runs counting zero at later boundaries; district evidence (fight-duration p50 and p90, storm-reach fraction, player storm-damage p50 and p90, storm share of incoming damage, split by first attempt, retry, win, and loss); a 12-cell starter matrix at 150 paired seeds per cell per mode with aggregate regression at most 3 points, cell regression at most 5 points, and 60 percent Quick and 40 percent Long floors; and 1,200 paired fresh profiles through three Quick runs with the D3, Quick-clear, feat, and Lantern protections. Storm levers are forbidden: no global storm timing change, no district storm override, no new combat input; any storm lever needs a separate approval and reservation, so this version measures storm and adjusts only the authorized power trial. The hasteMates change moves both azhdaha_v1 and azhdaha_v2, so 44 of the 46 aspects must hold the 0.100.0 zero-breach result and the two azhdaha aspects re-confirm in band. Trial constants, not final approval.
reservations.codexRuling: WAIT on source commit b094ae1a66d4d182ca43849d0559f58505601094, recorded 2026-07-18 and carried to canonical by the main integrator. Do not cherry-pick b094ae1, do not close 0.101.0, do not open follow-up versions; the reservation stays active. RATIFIED into the file set: tests/route.test.js (a necessary epoch-aware test) and handoff-back-difficulty-worker-0.101.0.md (the evidence hand-back); coordination/state.json carries the ruling records. Vizier 660 is NOT authorized: the paired starter evidence is not accepted while route-sim coverage labels Bull Market victory income blind, Overstocked shop behavior blind, shopping and fusion proxy, and hero shop pull proxy, with no live shop rolls, rerolls, or freeze decisions; the Kilnkeeper plus Bull Market 35 percent result is therefore not accepted as a real floor failure.
reservations.seamApproval: Codex approves the live-market seam proposal seam-proposal-live-market-0.101.0.md at worker commit 12e6d248f58509c84d916c5d4950382ca9d33c52, with the binding conditions in seamConditions. The reservation file set above is expanded accordingly (the seam proposal document plus the six implementation files it names). Implementation was blocked until canonical state carried this record and the worker branch received the identical reservation state; this commit is that correction. The difficulty worker may implement the seam strictly within the expanded file set and under the binding conditions. b094ae1 remains uningested on canonical and 0.101.0 remains open.
reservations.codexRuling2: WAIT, recorded 2026-07-18 on the seam implementation commit bf4f97697e06ddfb4c51309f49abc1916742886d and evidence hand-back 221832c. Do not cherry-pick either into launch-safe-ground; 0.101.0 stays open and the reservation active. PASSED: mode-specific coverage honest, extraction narrow with UI wrappers preserved, Bull income scoped to income only, frozen run.wareLock honored, 219 of 219 relevant tests independently reproduced, no Vizier, storm, targeting, golden, or additional difficulty change. BLOCKING: (1) the verifier's config-scoped nonGating design violates the approved exit-code contract (every failed gate must exit nonzero on every command); (2) the baseline artifact is not tied to a reproducible Git tree (uncommitted overlay; a user-supplied commit label is not tree verification; a computed seam-content hash with compare-time equality is required); (3) the candidate fails frozen Launch L2 gates, including a candidate-specific fresh-profile reach-D3 regression 95 to 81 percent that rejects the Quick D2 1.12 plus D3 1.18 pair (the paired baseline passes that gate, so it is not the player model). RATIFIED into the file set: tests/signature.test.js and handoff-back-seam-0.101.0.md. DIFFICULTY RULINGS: Quick D2 1.12 and D3 1.18 rejected as a pair; Long D5 2.10 may remain in the next diagnostic candidate; Long D6 held at 2.05; Azhdaha hasteMates 0.30 may remain; Vizier 660 still not authorized (the live Long matrix has about zero clear signal); storm levers still forbidden; the 60, 40, and 90 percent gates may not be lowered or reinterpreted after seeing failures without Robbie's explicit amendment. WORKER NEXT: restore the literal exit contract with a proving test; produce the baseline from a clean evidence commit based on 960e124; add a computed seam-content hash to both artifacts with required equality at compare; PROPOSE (not implement) a rollback-side Quick trial restoring run-1 district-3 reach to at least 90 percent, and a stronger behavior-specified market policy calibrated against observed play; leave Vizier, D6, storm, targeting, and combat semantics untouched. Codex signs only on clean candidate and comparison artifacts with all frozen gates passing, reproducible tree identities, the 2,400-seed trace, full tests, production build, browser and resume checks, and a final on-device storm-wall smoke.
reservations.codexRuling3: CORRECTED PACKET ACCEPTED, Launch L2 remains WAIT, recorded 2026-07-18. Codex verified: canonical HEAD 4f9b02b; neither bf4f976 nor 221832c in canonical ancestry; reservation open; both prior deviations ratified; evidence baseline 521e4cb clean and descending from 960e124; both trees independently compute seam hash 721465789eeacd7df219f21a6e765c69d1371006; regenerated artifacts behave correctly (baseline exit 1 with eight gating failures, candidate exit 1 with five, comparison exit 0 with all twelve paired gates passing); corrected verifier tests independently reproduced 12 of 12. The evidence infrastructure and live-market seam are ADMISSIBLE. This does not authorize integrating b094ae1: the candidate still fails the frozen Launch L2 gates. QUICK PROPOSAL: approved for DIAGNOSTIC EXECUTION ONLY, both evidence arms (T3 primary: Quick D2 held at baseline, D3 1.18; T1 control: D2 1.06, D3 1.12), under conditions: this ratification precedes execution; content-table overrides only with src/data.js unmodified; 1,200 paired fresh profiles per arm on identical seeds, seam hash, mp-1 policy, and baseline; EVERY existing gate reported, not only the three selection criteria; a passing arm is shape-admissible for further review only, never auto-authorized while other frozen gates fail; if neither arm passes, Quick power returns to baseline with no replacement trial in this version. MP-2: WAIT, remains a documented proposal; two corrections required before any implementation: (1) the frozen floors remain authoritative by default, a validated mp-2 gives Robbie evidence to consider an explicit amendment and creates no presumption of change; (2) the eligible telemetry corpus must be defined precisely (behavioral fitting may combine builds only when market rules and telemetry schema are proven identical; held-out clear-rate validation must use runs with the same difficulty constants and mode as the simulated baseline); the 30-run dependency is real and mp-2 must not be implemented or tuned against gate outcomes until the corpus exists. Vizier stays 700, Long D6 stays 2.05, storm timing unchanged, and no gameplay source may be edited until Codex reviews the diagnostic artifacts.
seamConditions: Coverage is mode-specific: the abstract policy's manifest rows remain proxy or blind; market:'live' rows may read FULL only where the live path is proven.
Exact offer order, offer ids, RNG draw count, enchant draws, freeze carryover, and rollIndex replay remain unchanged by the extraction.
Bull Market applies adjustedVictoryIncome only to incomeGold (board, relic, and charm income), never to base bounty gold.
Each run uses its frozen run.warePool snapshot (run.wareLock in code) for shop filtering, never the live profile mid-run.
No constant change and no Vizier change during the seam work.
reservations.closure: CLOSED 2026-07-19 on Robbie's explicit Launch L2 completion approval. Integrated at canonical merge a148a5866260a0349febac0c2d51f5a64256c2b9 (final worker commit a3d8145ec0c6ae7bd761bf4566d68d79e227549c). Final 0.101.0 content: Azhdaha hasteMates 0.30 on base, v1, v2 with the azhdaha_v2 integ 52 retune (parity-ledgered); Long D5 power 2.10; Long D6 held 2.05; Vizier held 700; Quick district power ROLLED BACK to the 0.100.0 baseline per the pre-registered diagnostic consequence (every trial arm failed the frozen gates; T3 falsified the D2 hypothesis by reproducing the full pair's 0.81 run-1 reach with D3 power alone); CONTENT_EPOCH 2 with frozen epoch-1 tables so active runs regenerate their own maps; the Launch L2 verifier under the literal exit contract with computed seam-content tree identity; the live-market seam (market-core extraction, mp-1 walker, mode-specific coverage); the quick-arms and quickLossChip diagnostic machinery. Closure evidence: npm test 559 green, production build, 60 browser checks with the resume replay, 2,400-seed trace zero breaches across 46 aspects, closure artifacts at C:/tmp/launch-l2-0.101.0-{baseline,candidate,comparison}.json (compare exit 0, all 12 paired gates, seam hash f12faf45d1db20a9a9df0488fc2dc3245f39cf8a, evidence commits 4a4a0a7 baseline tree and a3d8145 candidate tree). ACCEPTED DEVIATIONS, recorded under Robbie's completion authority: the frozen gates that remain failed at close are resolve-bands-quick and first-attempt-descends-quick (the measured structural tension: door-power levers cannot land the quick D4 Resolve band without breaking the 90 percent run-1 reach floor; the quickLossChip lever class is staged for a future version), first-attempt-descends-long D6 to D7 at 20 (the Vizier 660 trial was never authorized), and the starter-cell floors plus fresh-clear-by-run3 (model-scale artifacts of the abstract calibration, failing at both constant sets under the live policy; mp-2 recalibration remains a documented proposal blocked on its 30-run corpus). 0.101.0 strictly improves the frozen gate set versus 0.100.0: both gate-elite-spread failures removed, none added, worst paired cell delta zero. Not deployed: push and deploy to platosd.com remain a separate explicit approval.

launchL2Status: CLOSED 2026-07-19 on Robbie's explicit completion approval. Versions 0.99.4 (deployed), 0.100.0 (integrated), and 0.101.0 (integrated at a148a586) complete the calibrated-challenge arc: the dead hero-Omen pairing removed, the Board Aspect bands repaired and held at zero breaches, the Azhdaha snowball outlier fixed (Long Gate first-attempt 25 to about 53 percent), the Long D5 non-monotonic dip removed, the content-epoch machinery protecting active runs, and the evidence infrastructure (literal-contract verifier, live-market seam, tree-identity hashing) accepted by Codex as admissible. The accepted deviations and the staged next levers (quickLossChip Resolve pricing, mp-2 behavior calibration, the Vizier question) are recorded in the 0.101.0 reservation closure. Deploy of 0.100.0 plus 0.101.0 to platosd.com awaits Robbie's separate deploy approval.

launchL2Base.note: The canonical Launch L2 base is the closed Launch L1 head at product baseline 6d3ed77 (docs: log the After Midnight storm wall as a Launch L2 difficulty input), a verified ancestor of the launch-safe-ground HEAD. Launch L1 is CLOSED (see launchL1Status): all three versions integrated and deployed and the gate signed, and the After Midnight storm-wall difficulty input (see launchL2Inputs) is recorded on this base for the calibrated-challenge work. No combat, targeting, economy, map, or save semantics change to reach this base; the reconciliation is canonical-state only.

launchL1Status: CLOSED. All three Launch L1 versions (0.99.1 coordination, 0.99.2 content epoch, 0.99.3 route decisions) are integrated and deployed to https://platosd.com (origin/main). Codex signed the technical gate. Robbie's on-device smoke passed (full game playthrough, Long mode, Moneylender + Guild Charter, district 6 Ifrit loss, 4 gate retries, all checkpoints and decisions settled exactly once). Launch L1 exit criteria met. The closure carries forward unchanged as the Launch L2 base (see launchL2Base).

launchL2Inputs.source: Robbie on-device smoke 2026-07-18, run r1slbuqc at platosd.com
launchL2Inputs.owner: difficulty worker (0.101.0), Launch L2 calibrated challenge
launchL2Inputs.signal: Sharp difficulty cliff at the After Midnight bosses. The player cleared districts 1 through 4 at or near full health, then lost five straight attempts to the district 6 Ifrit boss and never dropped it below about 247 of 1537 health.
launchL2Inputs.rootCause: Storm timing, not board strength. stormAt was 16s but the late fights ran 24 to 26s, so storm dealt 378 to 550 unattributed damage per attempt and dominated the loss. The board (Silver icy Torch, Silver Sword, Gold swift Fire Bomb, Gold stout March, Silver venomous Writ, Salt Ward at tier 4) was adequate through the front half but could not close before the storm buried it.
launchL2Inputs.lever: The After Midnight power multiplier (2.05 at d6) stacked on top of the fixed storm arrival is the specific spike. The front half at power 1 gave no such pressure. Consider easing storm timing or the After Midnight multiplier in the back half, or telegraphing the storm clock so players build to beat it.
launchL2Inputs.relatedFinding: Reinforces the earlier real-playtest note that spikes come from the offensive-opening plus Dragon Gate pattern rather than from tier cost.

reservationLedger.verification: npm ci restored 52 packages from the committed lockfile with the lockfile unchanged; npm test passed 507 of 507 (13 new coordination tests); npm run build succeeded on vite 6.4.3.
reservationLedger.scope: Canonical coordination state, reservation validator, explicit staging in ship.js, and doc reconciliation (AGENTS.md tracked and reduced, CLAUDE.md and ROADMAP.md pointers, roadmap refinements R1 and R2).

reservationLedger.verification: npm test 515 of 515 (8 new content-epoch tests; byte-identical map and combat parity goldens hold); npm run build green; Playwright resume crash-recovery matrix 34 of 34 across both viewports; layout 26 of 28 (2 pre-existing stale Omen-count failures from 0.98.0, unrelated).
reservationLedger.scope: Content epoch decoupled from map retirement: CONTENT_EPOCH separate from MAP_VERSION and ROUTE_SAVE_VERSION, epoch-aware genMap, save v8 with migrateV7toV8, and readRouteSave retiring only on a map-structure mismatch.

reservationLedger.verification: Claude re-verified after cherry-pick: npm test 523 of 523; npm run build green; Playwright route-decisions recovery 2 of 2 and inherited resume matrix 34 of 34 across both viewports. Codex reported the same on its worktree. Robbie's on-device smoke passed: full playthrough Long mode with Moneylender+Charter, reached district 6, lost to Ifrit after 4 retries, all checkpoint and decision transactions settled exactly once. The seam changes no engine, data, map, or route-persistence file, so there is no combat, balance, or targeting change.
reservationLedger.scope: Behavior-preserving route decision transaction seam (src/route-decisions.js): a durable receipt freezes offers before display; commitRouteDecision validates before mutation, applies payment or reward, records the metric, advances the route, and marks the receipt in one synchronous transaction, with a duplicate guard for exact-once settlement across reload.

reservationLedger.verification: Verified on the dedicated worktree then re-verified after cherry-pick onto launch-safe-ground. Focused tests (node --test tests/unlock-omen.test.js tests/route-sim.test.js) 20 of 20, including six new: five in unlock-omen (compatibleOmenPool shape; a fresh Apothecary run never rolls Blood Moon at full unlock while the rest of the pool stays reachable; non-Apothecary fresh runs byte-identical to the pre-0.99.4 roll and Blood Moon still reachable; the exclusion consumes no rng so the cat shuffle stream is unchanged; a replay preserves an Apothecary plus Blood Moon run) and one negative-control guard in route-sim (Apothecary plus Blood Moon still constructible by explicit omenId). Full npm test 529 of 529 (523 baseline plus 6). npm run build green on vite 6.4.3 (814 modules). The change touches only the fresh run-start Omen draw and a pure helper, so combat parity goldens and every existing determinism pin hold unchanged.
reservationLedger.scope: Hero and Omen compatibility. compatibleOmenPool (pure, in src/unlock-profile.js, with the data block OMEN_HERO_INCOMPAT={apoth:['moon']}) drops a hero's incompatible Omens from the already-eligible pool in place order, skipping the drop when it would empty the pool. newRoute filters the unlocked eligible pool with it BEFORE the single deterministic draw, on the fresh run-start path only. No redraw loop and no extra rng draw: the draw still spends one rng() call, so every unaffected hero and Omen selection and the following shuffle stream are byte-identical. Explicit replay overrides keep the full unlocked pool and their recorded Omen overrides the roll; restored active runs never touch this draw; the simulator builds cells by explicit omenId, so Apothecary plus Blood Moon stays a negative-control cell. No combat, difficulty constant, src/data.js, targeting, economy, map, or save change.

reservationLedger.signedBy: Codex independently signed 0.100.0 on 2026-07-18 (gate 1 of the 0.101.0 start gate). Codex had also signed 0.99.4 before this version opened.
reservationLedger.verification: Data-only Board Aspect repair, verified on the dedicated worktree then re-verified after cherry-pick onto launch-safe-ground (source c09a4d6 and integration 6dbf27e are byte-identical trees). The before-to-after trace diff is exactly the eight target rows plus the summary count; all 38 other aspects, including controls rats_v2 (+0.0/-1.1), lamassu_v1 (+0.0/+5.7), and azhdaha_v1 (-2.4/-0.1), are byte-identical and in band. Focused aspects+parity+combat-trace 53 of 53; Quick route sim 1200 clear 70.6%, Long 56.4%, both 0 invalid runs, 0 guard trips, 0 fight timeouts; full npm test 529 of 529; production build green on vite 6.4.3 (814 modules). No engine, encounter, targeting, combat-semantics, map-generation, save-semantics, or difficulty-constant change; contentEpoch stays 1; combat goldens and parity ledgers untouched.
reservationLedger.scope: Reviewed candidate stat and board-order repairs to eight Board Aspects, data-only in src/data.js: matron_v2 regen 1->2, Venom Kiss poison 3->2, Grave Nail dmg 3->2; ghul_v2 both Rusted Hatchet cd 8->9; collector_v1 both Ledger Blade cd 8->8.5 integ 20->22; collector_v2 Counting Frame shield 8->3 cd 6->7 integ 14->6; peri_v2 both Shard Wings cd 2.5->2.6; golem_v2 both Coin Cannon cd 4->5; nasnas_v2 reordered to Bandage, Dagger, Vial, Torch, Buckler with shipped stats retained; simurgh_v1 Preen cd 8->9. Controls rats_v2, lamassu_v1, and azhdaha_v1 left unchanged.

namespace.ruling: Launch L1 through Launch L5 with the descriptive tokens launch-safe-ground, launch-calibrated-challenge, launch-market-agency, launch-route-choice-equity, and launch-release-experience. Historical content R8 keeps its name; no R8 code or test identifier is renamed. Never abbreviate a project to a bare L1 through L5, because the Lantern ladder already uses L1 through L10.

ownerSplit.ruling: Claude leads and integrates Launch L1 and implements 0.99.1 and 0.99.2. Codex reviews the frozen 0.99.2 integration, implements 0.99.3, and signs the Launch L1 exit gate. Codex leads Launch L3 and Launch L4. The difficulty worker owns 0.101.0. Claude leads Launch L5.

targetingReopen.ruling: Keep settled. No bulwark taunt or flying immunity reopen before 1.0. A later veto requires new reservations and renumbering.

signatureModel.ruling: Keep Model B. Unlocking a hero atomically unlocks both signature wares, and signatures stay outside the global Almanac chase. Fix presentation debt in 0.108.0. A veto requires a separately reserved progression migration version and renumbering.

displayName.ruling: Pending. Robbie decides, and it is due before 0.111.0 native metadata. Silence does not resolve it.

deploy.ruling: GitHub Pages only at platosd.com via git push origin main. Robbie approves each push at every deploy gate.

manorProduction.ruling: The game re-themes to the haunted Victorian manor (cozy-spooky, never gory) and visual production begins now, inserted before Launch L3 Market Agency, which shifts later along with Launch L4 and Launch L5. Display-layer pivot only via the established rename-ledger pattern: no engine, save, map, targeting, or economy change. Robbie granted Claude standing autonomy on the visual work; deploy still requires Robbie approval per the deploy ruling. The final display name stays a separate pending ruling.

reviewRulings.acceptance: Claude's frozen-target acceptance review returned ACCEPT for all five projects on 2026-07-18 against the roadmap at commit 15e8995.
reviewRulings.refinementR1: The full local dev dependency tree was absent, not only vite. It is restored with npm ci from the committed lockfile without changing the lockfile. Folded into the Launch L1 doc reconciliation.
reviewRulings.refinementR2: Bare L1 through L5 is forbidden because the Lantern ladder uses L1 through L10. Use the full Launch L labels in prose and the descriptive tokens in all machine readable state. Folded into the Launch L1 doc reconciliation.
